contentBlocks.original:
मार्कण्डेय उवाच
विशुद्धज्ञानदेहाय त्रिवेदीदिव्यचक्षुषे ।
श्रेयः प्राप्तिनिमित्ताय नमः सोमार्धधारिणे ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mārkaṇḍeya uvāca .
oṃ viśuddhajñānadehāya trivedīdivyacakṣuṣe .
śreyaḥprāptinimittāya namaḥ somārdhadhāriṇe .. 1 ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Sage Markandeya said: (I offer my salutations to the one) the essence of whose form is that of pure knowledge (of absolute consciousness) [vishuddha-jnana-dehaya] and whose three divine eyes form the three vedas [tri-vedi-divya-cakssusse],
to imbibe that auspiciousness (of pure knowledge). I meditate on Shiva and Shakti in their conscious blissful form. I offer my salutations to Shiva who bears the half-of-soma (soma means moon) and also to Shakti who bears half-of-soma (in her body as ardhanareeswara. Soma here is Shiva). Therefore, she has the same qualities of Shiva, of visuddha jnana deha and trivedi divya cakssu. Keelaka is pin or nail. The conscious form of Shakti is pinned to all the mantras of devi mahatmyam as the underlying principle through the keelaka stotram.
contentBlocks.meanings: मार्कण्डेय (mārkaṇḍeya) – Markandeya; उवाच (uvāca) – said; विशुद्धज्ञानदेहाय (viśuddhajñānadehāya) – to the one with pure knowledge form (viśuddha:pure | jñāna:knowledge | dehāya:to the form); त्रिवेदीदिव्यचक्षुषे (trivedīdivyacakṣuṣe) – to the one with three divine eyes (trivedī:three vedas | divya:divine | cakṣuṣe:to the eyes); श्रेयः (śreyaḥ) – auspiciousness; प्राप्तिनिमित्ताय (prāptinimittāya) – for the attainment reason (prāpti:attainment | nimittāya:for the reason); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; सोमार्धधारिणे (somārdhadhāriṇe) – to the bearer of half-moon (soma:moon | ardha:half | dhāriṇe:to the bearer);

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वमेतद् विजानीयान्मन्त्राणामपि कीलकम् ।
सोऽपि क्षेममवाप्नोति सततं जाप्यतत्परः ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvametadvijānīyānmantrāṇāmapi kīlakam .
so'pi kṣemamavāpnoti satataṃ japyatatparaḥ .. 2 ..
contentBlocks.translation: All these hymns of Keelaka stotram, one should know, are indeed the keelaka (pin or nail) by which the mantras (of devi mahatmyan are pinned) (i.e. by which the underlying principle of the conscious form of devi are pinned), he who devotes himself to continuously chant these hymns, indeed attains tranquility of mind (i.e. he who continuously meditates on the conscious form of devi in the heart indeed attains tranquility of mind).
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वम् (sarvam) – all; एतद् (etad) – this; विजानीयात् (vijānīyāt) – should know; मन्त्राणाम् (mantrāṇām) – of the mantras; अपि (api) – also; कीलकम् (kīlakam) – pin or nail; सः (saḥ) – he; अपि (api) – also; क्षेमम् (kṣemam) – tranquility; अवाप्नोति (avāpnoti) – attains; सततम् (satatam) – continuously; जाप्यतत्परः (jāpyatatparaḥ) – devoted to chanting (jāpya:chanting | tatparaḥ:devoted to);

contentBlocks.original:
सिद्ध्यन्त्युच्चाटनादीनि कर्माणि सकलान्यपि ।
एतेन स्तुवतां देविं स्तोत्रवृन्देन भक्तितः ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
siddhyantyuccāṭanādīni karmāṇi sakalānyapi .
etena stuvatāṃ devīṃ stotravṛndena bhaktitaḥ .. 3 ..
contentBlocks.translation: He accomplishes uccatana (eradication of inner enemies) to the very root, and all related (spiritual) accomplishments, by extolling the devi with devotional fervour with this collection of hymns.
contentBlocks.meanings: सिद्ध्यन्ति (siddhyanti) – accomplish; उच्चाटनादीनि (uccāṭanādīni) – eradication and others (uccāṭana:eradication | ādīni:and others); कर्माणि (karmāṇi) – actions; सकलानि (sakalāni) – all; अपि (api) – also; एतेन (etena) – by this; स्तुवताम् (stuvatām) – of those praising; देविम् (devīm) – the goddess; स्तोत्रवृन्देन (stotravṛndena) – with a collection of hymns (stotra:hymn | vṛndena:with a collection); भक्तितः (bhaktitaḥ) – with devotion;

contentBlocks.original:
न मन्त्रो नौषधं तत्र न किञ्चिदपि विद्यते ।
विना जप्येन सिद्धयेत्तु सर्वमुच्चाटनादिकम् ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na mantro nauṣadhaṃ tasya na kiñcidapi vidyate .
vinā japyena siddhyettu sarvamuccāṭanādikam .. 4 ..
contentBlocks.translation: There are no mantras (hymns) or ousadha (medicine) available, even a trace (which can accomplish uccatana), without chanting (any mantras) one accomplishes uccatana (eradication of inner enemies), all to its very root (by meditating on the conscious form of devi in the heart while reciting the devi mahatmyam).(here reciting devi mahatmyam is not like chanting any mantra to obtain the fruit of uccatana, but for meditation on the conscious form of devi and merging our ego in her.)
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; मन्त्रः (mantraḥ) – hymn; न (na) – not; औषधं (auṣadham) – medicine; तत्र (tatra) – there; न (na) – not; किञ्चिदपि (kiñcidapi) – even a trace (kiñcit:a little | api:even); विद्यते (vidyate) – exists; विना (vinā) – without; जप्येन (japyena) – by chanting; सिद्ध्येत् (siddhyet) – accomplishes; तु (tu) – but; सर्वम् (sarvam) – all; उच्चाटनादिकम् (uccāṭanādikam) – eradication and others (uccāṭana:eradication | ādikam:and others);

contentBlocks.original:
समग्राण्यपि सेत्स्यन्ति लोकशङ्कामिमां हरः ।
कृत्वा निमन्त्रयामास सर्वमेवमिदं शुभम् ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samagrāṇyapi setsyanti lokaśaṅkāmimāṃ haraḥ .
kṛtvā nimantrayāmāsa sarvamevamidaṃ śubham .. 5 ..
contentBlocks.translation: People had this doubt, how can it be that everything is indeed accomplished (uccatana and other fruits) by reciting the devi mahatmyam which appears to be full of violence; Lord Shiva summoned and pinned the underlying principle of the conscious form of devi to all the mantras of devi mahatmyam; and having done that all this indeed became auspicious (i.e. now the violence in the battlefield became the auspicious force purging us from within).
contentBlocks.meanings: समग्राणि (samagrāṇi) – all; अपि (api) – also; सेत्स्यन्ति (setsyanti) – will be accomplished; लोकशङ्काम् (lokaśaṅkām) – doubt of the people (loka:people | śaṅkām:doubt); इमाम् (imām) – this; हरः (haraḥ) – Lord Shiva; कृत्वा (kṛtvā) – having done; निमन्त्रयामास (nimantrayāmāsa) – summoned; सर्वम् (sarvam) – all; एवम् (evam) – thus; इदं (idaṃ) – this; शुभम् (śubham) – auspicious;

contentBlocks.original:
स्तोत्रं वै चण्डिकायास्तु तच्च गुह्यं चकार सः ।
समाप्नोति स पुण्येन तां यथावन्निमन्त्रणाम् ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stotraṃ vai caṇḍikāyāstu tacca guhyaṃ cakāra saḥ .
samāpnoti sa puṇyena tāṃ yathāvannimantraṇām .. 6 ..
contentBlocks.translation: That stotra of devi Chandika (i.e. Keelaka stotra), lord Shiva indeed made it hidden (behind every mantra of devi mahatmyam) thus making the conscious form of devi as the object of meditation, he achieves everything by her auspiciousness, when she is invoked in the right manner by meditating on her eternal conscious form.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्तोत्रं (stotraṃ) – hymn; वै (vai) – indeed; चण्डिकायाः (caṇḍikāyāḥ) – of Caṇḍikā; तु (tu) – but; तत् (tat) – that; च (ca) – and; गुह्यं (guhyaṃ) – secret; चकार (cakāra) – made; सः (saḥ) – he; समाप्नोति (samāpnoti) – achieves; स (sa) – he; पुण्येन (puṇyena) – by auspiciousness; तां (tāṃ) – her; यथावत् (yathāvat) – right manner; निमन्त्रणाम् (nimantraṇām) – invocation;

contentBlocks.original:
सोऽपि क्षेममवाप्नोति सर्वमेव न संशयः ।
कृष्णायां वा चतुर्दश्यामष्टम्यां वा समाहितः ॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
so'pi kṣemamavāpnoti sarvameva na saṃśayaḥ .
kṛṣṇāyāṃ vā caturdaśyāmaṣṭamyāṃ vā samāhitaḥ .. 7 ..
contentBlocks.translation: He indeed attains tranquility of mind and all other fruits, there is no doubt about it, he who meditates (on the devi) on Krishna paksha chaturdashi or ashtami of a month.
contentBlocks.meanings: सः (saḥ) – he; अपि (api) – indeed; क्षेमम् (kṣemam) – tranquility; अवाप्नोति (avāpnoti) – attains; सर्वम् (sarvam) – all; एव (eva) – indeed; न (na) – no; संशयः (saṃśayaḥ) – doubt; कृष्णायाम् (kṛṣṇāyām) – on Krishna paksha (kṛṣṇa:Krishna | āyām:on); वा (vā) – or; चतुर्दश्याम् (caturdaśyām) – on the fourteenth (catur:four | daśyām:tenth); अष्टम्याम् (aṣṭamyām) – on the eighth (aṣṭa:eight | myām:on); वा (vā) – or; समाहितः (samāhitaḥ) – meditates;

contentBlocks.original:
ददाति प्रतिगृह्णाति नान्यथैषा प्रसीदति ।
इत्थंरूपेण कीलेन महादेवेन कीलितम् ॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dadāti pratigṛhṇāti nānyathaiṣā prasīdati .
itthaṃ rūpeṇa kīlena mahādevena kīlitam .. 8 ..
contentBlocks.translation: While meditating on the conscious form of devi during recitation of devi mahatmyam, he should give himself in surrender to her lotus feet, and receive in return her grace; Otherwise she is not pleased (by mechanical recitation of devi mahatmyam bereft of devotion), in this manner, by the keela (pin of keelaka stotram) Mahadeva pinned the mantras of devi mahatmyam (making the meditation on the conscious form of devi the central theme).
contentBlocks.meanings: ददाति (dadāti) – gives; प्रतिगृह्णाति (pratigṛhṇāti) – receives; न (na) – not; अन्यथा (anyathā) – otherwise; एषा (eṣā) – she; प्रसीदति (prasīdati) – is pleased; इत्थं (itthaṃ) – in this manner; रूपेण (rūpeṇa) – by form; कीलेन (kīlena) – by the pin; महादेवेन (mahādevena) – by Mahadeva (mahā:great | devena:by the god); कीलितम् (kīlitam) – pinned;

contentBlocks.original:
यो निष्कीलां विधायैनां चण्डीं जपति नित्यशः ।
स सिद्धः स गणः सोऽथ गन्धर्वो जायते ध्रुवम् ॥९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yo niṣkīlāṃ vidhāyaināṃ caṇḍīṃ japati nityaśaḥ .
sa siddhaḥ sa gaṇaḥ so'tha gandharvo jāyate dhruvam .. 9 ..
contentBlocks.translation: He who after unpinning the keelaka makes her manifest (i.e. invokes her conscious form in the heart), and then recites the chandi, by constantly dwelling (on her conscious form), he becomes a siddha (spiritually accomplished), he becomes a gana (an attendent of the devi), he becomes a gandharva (celestial singer who glorifies the devi), truly.
contentBlocks.meanings: यः (yaḥ) – he who; निष्कीलाम् (niṣkīlām) – unpinning; विधाय (vidhāya) – making; एनाम् (enām) – her; चण्डीम् (caṇḍīm) – Chandi; जपति (japati) – recites; नित्यशः (nityaśaḥ) – constantly; स (sa) – he; सिद्धः (siddhaḥ) – accomplished; स (sa) – he; गणः (gaṇaḥ) – attendant; सः (saḥ) – he; अथ (atha) – then; गन्धर्वः (gandharvaḥ) – celestial singer; जायते (jāyate) – becomes; ध्रुवम् (dhruvam) – truly;

contentBlocks.original:
न चैवापाटवं तस्य भयं क्वापि न जायते ।
नापमृत्युवशं याति मृते च मोक्षमाप्नुयात् ॥१०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na caivāpāṭavaṃ tasya bhayaṃ kvāpi na jāyate .
nāpamṛtyuvaśaṃ yāti mṛte ca mokṣamāpnuyāt .. 10 ..
contentBlocks.translation: He indeed does not become handicapped (prematurely) with major diseases; fear does not rise in him anywhere (which leads to unnecessary anxiety), he does not become victim of premature death; and after death attains moksha (liberation).
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; च (ca) – and; एव (eva) – indeed; अपाटवं (apāṭavam) – handicapped; तस्य (tasya) – his; भयम् (bhayam) – fear; क्वापि (kvāpi) – anywhere; न (na) – not; जायते (jāyate) – arises; न (na) – not; अपमृत्युवशम् (apamṛtyuvaśam) – premature death (apa:premature | mṛtyu:death | vaśam:control); याति (yāti) – goes; मृते (mṛte) – after death; च (ca) – and; मोक्षम् (mokṣam) – liberation; आप्नुयात् (āpnuyāt) – attains;

contentBlocks.original:
ज्ञात्वा प्रारभ्य कुर्वीत ह्यकुर्वाणो विनश्यति ।
ततो ज्ञात्वैव सम्पूर्णमिदं प्रारभ्यते बुधैः ॥११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jñātvā prārabhya kurvīta hyakurvāṇo vinaśyati .
tato jñātvaiva sampūrṇamidaṃ prārabhyate budhaiḥ .. 11 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Only after knowing (the keelakam), he should begin (reciting the devi mahatmyam); if not done in this manner, he is deprived (of the fruits of recitation), therefore only after knowing this fully (i.e. understanding the keelaka fully), the wise men begin reciting the devi mahatmyam.
contentBlocks.meanings: ज्ञात्वा (jñātvā) – having known; प्रारभ्य (prārabhya) – beginning; कुर्वीत (kurvīta) – should do; हि (hi) – indeed; अकुर्वाणः (akurvāṇaḥ) – not doing; विनश्यति (vinaśyati) – perishes; ततः (tataḥ) – therefore; ज्ञात्वा (jñātvā) – having known; एव (eva) – only; सम्पूर्णम् (sampūrṇam) – fully; इदं (idaṃ) – this; प्रारभ्यते (prārabhyate) – is begun; बुधैः (budhaiḥ) – by the wise;

contentBlocks.original:
सौभाग्यादि च यत्किञ्चिद् दृश्यते ललनाजने ।
तत्सर्वं तत्प्रसादेन तेन जप्यमिदं शुभम् ॥१२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saubhāgyādi ca yatkiñcid dṛśyate lalanājane .
tatsarvaṃ tatprasādena tena japyamidaṃ śubham .. 12 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whatever good fortune, beauty, charm and the like are seen in women, all that is the result of your grace; Therefore women should chant this auspicious stotra and meditate on the auspicious form of the devi.
contentBlocks.meanings: सौभाग्यादि (saubhāgyādi) – good fortune and more (saubhāgya:good fortune | ādi:and more); च (ca) – and; यत्किञ्चित् (yatkiñcit) – whatever; दृश्यते (dṛśyate) – is seen; ललनाजने (lalanājane) – in women (lalana:women | jane:in people); तत्सर्वं (tatsarvaṃ) – all that (tat:that | sarvaṃ:all); तत्प्रसादेन (tatprasādena) – by your grace (tat:your | prasādena:by grace); तेन (tena) – therefore; जप्यम् (japyam) – should be chanted; इदं (idaṃ) – this; शुभम् (śubham) – auspicious;

contentBlocks.original:
शनैस्तु जप्यमानेऽस्मिन् स्तोत्रे सम्पत्तिरुच्चकैः ।
भवत्येव समग्रापि ततः प्रारभ्यमेव तत् ॥१३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śanaistu japyamāne'smin stotre sampattiruccakaiḥ .
bhavatyeva samagrāpi tataḥ prārabhyameva tat .. 13 ..
contentBlocks.translation: If this stotra is chanted gently (without haste) (meditating on its true import), one attains (spiritual) wealth of the highest kind, one indeed becomes the whole (i.e. fragmentation of the ego gradually melts and one feels the conscious form of devi behind everything); therefore, one should undertake the chanting of this keelaka.
contentBlocks.meanings: शनैः (śanaiḥ) – gently; तु (tu) – but; जप्यमाने (japyamāne) – being chanted; अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; स्तोत्रे (stotre) – hymn; सम्पत्तिः (sampattiḥ) – wealth; उच्चकैः (uccakaiḥ) – of the highest kind; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; एव (eva) – indeed; समग्रापि (samagrāpi) – whole (samagrā:complete | api:also); ततः (tataḥ) – therefore; प्रारभ्यम् (prārabhyam) – should be undertaken; एव (eva) – indeed; तत् (tat) – that;

contentBlocks.original:
ऐश्वर्यं यत्प्रसादेन सौभाग्यारोग्यसम्पदः ।
शत्रुहानिः परो मोक्षः स्तूयते सा न किं जनैः ॥१४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aiśvaryaṃ tatprasādena saubhāgyārogyameva ca .
śatruhāniḥ paro mokṣaḥ stūyate sā na kiṃ janaiḥ .. 14 ..
contentBlocks.translation: By whose grace divine qualities (aishwaryam) are awakened; by whose presence good fortune (saubhagya), health (freedom from disease) (arogya) and wealth (sampada) are manifested, by whose mercy enemies are eradicated; (and) by whose special grace one finally attains the highest liberation (moksha); why should'nt she be praised by the people (who is all-merciful)?
contentBlocks.meanings: ऐश्वर्यं (aiśvaryaṃ) – divine qualities; यत् (yat) – by whose; प्रसादेन (prasādena) – by grace; सौभाग्य (saubhāgya) – good fortune; आरोग्य (ārogya) – health; सम्पदः (sampadaḥ) – wealth; शत्रुहानिः (śatruhāniḥ) – eradication of enemies (śatru:enemy | hāniḥ:eradication); परः (paraḥ) – highest; मोक्षः (mokṣaḥ) – liberation; स्तूयते (stūyate) – is praised; सा (sā) – she; न (na) – not; किं (kiṃ) – why; जनैः (janaiḥ) – by people;

contentBlocks.original:
चण्डिकां हृदयेनापि यः स्मरेत् सततं नरः ।
हृद्यं काममवाप्नोति हृदि देवी सदा वसेत् ॥१५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
caṇḍikāṃ hṛdayenāpi yaḥ smaret satataṃ naraḥ .
hṛdyaṃ kāmamavāpnoti hṛdi devī sadā vaset .. 15 ..
contentBlocks.translation: The person who constantly remembers (the conscious form of) devi Chandika with devotion, attains his innermost longing; and devi always dwells in his heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: चण्डिकाम् (caṇḍikām) – Chandika; हृदयेन (hṛdayena) – with the heart; अपि (api) – also; यः (yaḥ) – who; स्मरेत् (smaret) – remembers; सततम् (satatam) – constantly; नरः (naraḥ) – man; हृद्यम् (hṛdyam) – innermost; कामम् (kāmam) – desire; अवाप्नोति (avāpnoti) – attains; हृदि (hṛdi) – in the heart; देवी (devī) – goddess; सदा (sadā) – always; वसेत् (vaset) – dwells;

contentBlocks.original:
अग्रतोऽमुं महादेवकृतं कीलकवारणम् ।
निष्किलञ्च तथा कृत्वा पठितव्यं समाहितैः ॥१६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
agrato'muṃ mahādevakṛtaṃ kīlakavāraṇam .
niṣkīlañca tathā kṛtvā paṭhitavyaṃ samāhitaiḥ .. 16 ..
contentBlocks.translation: First of all, the preventive measure of keelaka created by Mahadeva (i.e. the pin of keelaka) should be unpinned, and then after doing that one should recite (the devi mahatmyam) with concentration (meditating on the conscious form of the devi).
contentBlocks.meanings: अग्रतः (agrataḥ) – in front; अम् (am) – this; महादेवकृतम् (mahādevakṛtam) – created by Mahadeva (mahādeva:Mahadeva | kṛtam:created); कीलकवारणम् (kīlakavāraṇam) – preventive measure of keelaka (kīlaka:keelaka | vāraṇam:preventive measure); निष्कीलम् (niṣkīlam) – unpinned; च (ca) – and; तथा (tathā) – then; कृत्वा (kṛtvā) – having done; पठितव्यम् (paṭhitavyam) – should be recited; समाहितैः (samāhitaiḥ) – with concentration;
